connections.name: DisplayPort 1.2 + USB 3.0
requirements: DisplayPort 1.2 output (or Mini DP with adapter)

knownIssues.title: Discontinued in 2021 — legacy Oculus runtime only
knownIssues.description: The Rift S was discontinued and the Oculus PC runtime that supports it is legacy (v40+). New features target Quest Link instead; don't expect bug fixes.
knownIssues.workaround: Keep the legacy Oculus PC app installer archived. If a Windows update breaks it, a clean reinstall of the legacy version is often the only recovery path.

knownIssues.title: 80 Hz refresh rate only — no higher-Hz mode
knownIssues.description: Unlike Quest headsets, the Rift S display is locked at 80 Hz. You cannot run 90 Hz or 120 Hz modes even if your PC can drive them.
knownIssues.workaround: Target a 12.5 ms frame budget. Enable ASW to drop cleanly to 40 Hz reprojected when your GPU can't hit 80 Hz natively.

optimizationTips.tip: Target exactly 80 FPS — anything more is wasted
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: The Rift S runs at a fixed 80 Hz. Any frames beyond 80 FPS are discarded — keep your in-app graphics settings maxed out to make the most of every frame.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: With a locked 80 Hz refresh and ASW available, the ideal GPU profile is 80+ FPS sustained, drop to ~40 FPS + ASW as a fallback. There's no benefit to higher frame rates. Use the Oculus Debug Tool's frame time graph (Ctrl+numpad 1) to monitor compositor drops.

optimizationTips.tip: ASW (Asynchronous Spacewarp) is your safety net
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: When your PC can't keep up, ASW generates in-between frames so VR still feels smooth. Keep it enabled in Oculus Debug Tool.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Oculus Debug Tool → ASW → Auto (default). When native rendering drops below 80 FPS, ASW engages at 40 FPS and reprojects missing frames. Tearing artifacts during rapid motion are ASW's known failure mode — preferable to hard stutter.
